events.text: Elizabeth Moore, 60-year-old White female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the Head of pancreas. Clinical staging: T2N1M0, AJCC Stage IIB. ICD-10: C25.9. Stage IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, borderline resectable.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 neuropathy. Dose reduction of neurotoxic agent by 25%. Gabapentin 300 mg TID started.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.

Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: mFOLFIRINOX (Oxaliplatin 85 mg/m2 + Irinotecan 150 mg/m2 + Leucovorin 400 mg/m2 + 5-FU 2400 mg/m2 46h infusion) q2w x 8 cycles. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

Underwent surgical resection: Pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with portal vein resection and reconstruction. Estimated blood loss 250 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated alternative first-line: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 + Nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated alternative first-line: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 + Nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated alternative first-line: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 + Nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated alternative first-line: Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 + Nab-paclitaxel 125 mg/m2 days 1, 8, 15 q4w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
PET/CT performed for restaging after alternative first-line.
Clinical visit for results review after alternative first-line.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
MRI ABDOMEN WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Elizabeth Moore
MRN: patient_337cd49d8f9f
Date of Study: 2023-03-31

CLINICAL INDICATION: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
Unremarkable examination.

IMPRESSION:
1. Pancreatic head mass abutting the SMV. Borderline resectable.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Elizabeth Moore
MRN: patient_337cd49d8f9f
Specimen ID: S2023-26943
Date of Procedure: 2023-04-03
Date of Report: 2023-04-08

SPECIMEN: Head of pancreas, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.4 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
MUC1: Positive
CDX2: Negative
SMAD4: Lost (absent staining)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Elizabeth Moore - Head of pancreas" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 6.4 x 4.5 x 3.2 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A6.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Sarah Chen, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Elizabeth Moore
MRN: patient_337cd49d8f9f
Date: 2023-04-11
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Moore is a 60-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas, IIB. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, borderline resectable. Pathology confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obesity
- COPD

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 10 years ago)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Engineer
- Lives with partner

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of cancer.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: intermittent nausea, mild dyspnea on exertion. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 119/72, HR 80, RR 19, Temp 98.7F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 58.7 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.7, Hgb 9.9, Plt 217, Cr 1.15

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
60-year-old female with IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas. initial oncology consultation.

1. Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas, Stage IIB
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Peripheral access adequate
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Elizabeth Moore
MRN: patient_337cd49d8f9f
Date of Report: 2023-04-17
Specimen: Head of pancreas (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 4.7 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) - Missense (Exon 2) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.G245S - Missense (Exon 7)
  CDKN2A Loss - Homozygous deletion
  SMAD4 p.R361H - Missense (Exon 8)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  BRCA2 p.T3085A - VUS
  PALB2 p.L1009fs - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - KRAS G12D: Investigational KRAS G12D inhibitors in clinical trials.
  - BRCA/PALB2 VUS noted - genetic counseling recommended.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 756x coverage.
Tumor content: 64%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Hepatobiliary Surgery

Date: 2023-05-13

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Moore is a 60-year-old female with IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient reports feeling well overall. Weight is stable.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild dyspnea on exertion, intermittent nausea, mild cough, decreased appetite. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 121/72, HR 69, RR 16, Temp 98.3F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 59.1 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.2, Hgb 13.0, Plt 241, Cr 1.14

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
60-year-old female with IIB pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma - cycle 2 assessment
   - Restaging imaging to be scheduled
   - Monitoring for treatment-related toxicity
2. Symptom management
   - Continue antiemetic regimen
3. Follow-up in 6 weeks

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Elizabeth Moore
MRN: patient_337cd49d8f9f
Date of Study: 2023-08-01

CLINICAL INDICATION: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of the head of pancreas. restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: No hepatic metastases identified.

PANCREAS: Hypodense mass in the head of the pancreas measuring 3.0 x 2.1 cm with encasement of the SMV. Pancreatic duct dilated to 6 mm.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Pancreatic head mass with vascular involvement. Borderline resectable.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Elizabeth Moore
MRN: patient_337cd49d8f9f
Specimen ID: S2023-44100
Date of Procedure: 2023-08-23
Date of Report: 2023-08-27

SPECIMEN: Head of pancreas, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.3 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Negative
MUC1: Positive
CDX2: Negative
SMAD4: Lost (absent staining)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Elizabeth Moore - Head of pancreas" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 2.3 x 1.6 x 1.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology
